text: Machine learning is a way for computers to learn from data. In supervised learning, we give the computer training data that includes both inputs and correct outputs. For example, we might show it pictures of cats and dogs, each labeled. The computer uses this data to build a model, which is like a set of rules for making decisions. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions on new, unseen data. To check how well the model works, we measure its accuracy. A high accuracy means the model is good at predicting correctly, while low accuracy suggests it needs improvement.
